---
title: "Eutrophication-agricultural Analysis for Maumee River/Lake Erie"
author: "FL"
output:
  html_document:
    code_folding: hide
    toc: yes
    toc_depth: 3
    toc_float: yes
editor_options: 
  markdown: 
    wrap: 72
---


```{r setup, include=FALSE}
#knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = F)
options(knitr.kable.NA = '')  # replace NA with blank
```

<!-- Load package -->

<!-- shortcut: -->

<!-- insert R chuck:  ctrl+alt+I -->

<!-- knit the file:  ctrl+shift+K -->

<!-- run current chuck: Ctrl+shift+enter  -->


## 1) Install/load packages

```{r, warning=FALSE,message=FALSE, include=T}
# load packages
#install.packages(c("dataRetrieval","dplyr","lubridate","ggplot2","readr","stringr","tidyr"))
library(dataRetrieval)
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)
library(ggplot2)
library(stringr)
library(tidyr)
library(tidyverse)
```


### 2) Get Maumee watershed polygons (USGS WBD) in R

```{r}
library(httr)
library(sf)
library(jsonlite)
```


```{r}
wbd <- "https://hydro.nationalmap.gov/arcgis/rest/services/wbd/MapServer"

# HUC8 layer is commonly 4 in this service; you can confirm in the MapServer directory.
layer_id <- 4

# Pull HUC8 features whose name includes "Maumee" (may return multiple features)
res <- GET(
  paste0(wbd, "/", layer_id, "/query"),
  query = list(
    where = "Name LIKE '%Maumee%'",
    outFields = "*",
    returnGeometry = "true",
    f = "geojson"
  )
)

maumee_huc8 <- st_read(content(res, as="text", encoding="UTF-8"), quiet = TRUE)
st_write(maumee_huc8, "maumee_huc8.geojson", delete_dsn = TRUE)
```

### 2) Land use / crops: download CDL for your AOI using CropScapeR

GetCDLData() can take an sf AOI and (for type = "b") automatically uses the AOIâ€™s bounding box to request a GeoTIFF from CropScape.


```{r}
library(CropScapeR)
library(terra)
library(sf)
```


```{r}
ws <- st_read("maumee_huc8.geojson", quiet = TRUE)

# Download CDL for the AOI bounding box (fast + reliable)
cdl_2024 <- GetCDLData(
  aoi = ws,
  year = 2024,
  type = "b",
  save_path = "cdl_2024_bbox.tif",
  readr = TRUE
)

# Clip to watershed polygon
r <- rast("cdl_2024_bbox.tif")
ws_r <- vect(st_transform(ws, crs(r)))
cdl_clip <- mask(crop(r, ws_r), ws_r)
writeRaster(cdl_clip, "cdl_2024_maumee.tif", overwrite = TRUE)
```

### 3) Crop acreage by class inside the watershed (from CDL raster)


```{r}
library(terra)
library(dplyr)
```


```{r}

cdl <- rast("cdl_2024_maumee.tif")

# If your raster is in lon/lat (degrees), project to an equal-area CRS first
if (is.lonlat(cdl)) {
  cdl <- project(cdl, "EPSG:5070", method="near")  # NAD83 / Conus Albers (good for area)
}

cell_ha <- cellSize(cdl, unit = "ha")
cell_ac <- cell_ha * 2.47105381

```


### Crop distribution: acres + percent by CDL class Compute acres by CDL code

```{r}
library(dplyr)

# Make sure NA cells are masked out
cell_ac <- cellSize(cdl, unit="ha") * 2.47105381

v  <- values(cdl, mat=FALSE)
ac <- values(cell_ac, mat=FALSE)

tab <- tibble(class = v, acres = ac) |>
  filter(!is.na(class)) |>
  group_by(class) |>
  summarise(acres = sum(acres, na.rm=TRUE), .groups="drop") |>
  arrange(desc(acres))

tab |> head(5)
```




```{r}
# this is most recent data for 2025
crop_lut <- tibble(
  class = c(1, 5, 24, 4, 28, 36, 37, 41, 42),
  crop  = c("Corn", "Soybeans", "Winter Wheat", "Sorghum",
            "Oats", "Alfalfa", "Other Hay", "Sugarbeets", "Dry Beans")
)

main <- tab |>
  inner_join(crop_lut, by="class") |>
  mutate(percent = 100 * acres / sum(acres)) |>
  arrange(desc(acres))

main
```

**Note**: Fertilizer Needs of Soybean, Corn, Wheat, and Alfalfa
Soybean, corn, wheat, and alfalfa require phosphorus fertilizers, but the amount needed can vary based on soil test results and the specific nutrient requirements of the crops.


3) â€śTop cropsâ€ť including anything not in your lookup (optional)

This gives you the top N classes by acres, whether labeled or not:

```{r}
topN <- 15
top <- tab |>
  mutate(percent = 100 * acres / sum(acres)) |>
  slice_head(n = topN)

top

barplot(main$percent, names.arg = main$crop, las=2,
        ylab="Percent of cropped area (within watershed)",
        main="Main crops in Maumee watershed (CDL)")

```

###  to get the crops types for the top crops from 2008 to current:

```{r}
# ============================================================
# CDL crop time series for a watershed (Maumee) â€” 2008 to present
# Crops: Corn, Soybeans, Winter Wheat, Alfalfa
# - Downloads CDL GeoTIFFs via CropScapeR (bbox of watershed)
# - Clips to watershed, computes acres + percent for each crop
# - Saves CSV (long + wide) and makes a trend plot
# ============================================================

library(sf)
library(terra)
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(tidyr)
library(CropScapeR)
library(ggplot2)

# ----------------------------
# USER INPUTS
# ----------------------------
watershed_file <- "maumee_huc8.geojson"     # watershed polygon (GeoJSON/Shapefile)
out_dir <- "cdl_maumee_timeseries"          # output folder
start_year <- 2008
```


```{r}
# ----------------------------
# SETUP
# ----------------------------
dir.create(out_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
dir.create(file.path(out_dir, "rasters_bbox"), showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

# Equal-area CRS for accurate area
aea <- "EPSG:5070"

# Read watershed and project to equal-area
ws_sf <- st_read(watershed_file, quiet = TRUE) |>
  st_make_valid() |>
  st_transform(aea)

ws_vect <- vect(ws_sf)

# Crop lookup (CDL class codes)
crop_lut <- tibble(
  cdl_code = c(1, 5, 24, 36),
  crop     = c("Corn", "Soybeans", "Winter Wheat", "Alfalfa")
)

# Year range
this_year <- as.integer(format(Sys.Date(), "%Y")) #2025 , up to present 
years <- start_year:this_year
```


```{r}
# ----------------------------
# FUNCTION: compute crop acres for one year
# ----------------------------
crops_one_year <- function(year, ws_sf_aea, ws_vect_aea, crop_lut, out_dir) {

  tif_bbox <- file.path(out_dir, "rasters_bbox", paste0("cdl_", year, "_bbox.tif"))

  # 1) Download once (cache)
  if (!file.exists(tif_bbox)) {
    message("Downloading CDL for ", year, " ...")
    ok <- tryCatch({
      GetCDLData(
        aoi = ws_sf_aea,
        year = year,
        type = "b",              # bbox of AOI
        save_path = tif_bbox,
        readr = FALSE
      )
      TRUE
    }, error = function(e) {
      message("  Failed to download year ", year, ": ", conditionMessage(e))
      FALSE
    })

    if (!ok) {
      return(tibble(
        year = year, crop = crop_lut$crop,
        acres = NA_real_, percent_of_watershed = NA_real_
      ))
    }
  } else {
    message("Using cached CDL for ", year)
  }

  # 2) Read raster
  r <- tryCatch(rast(tif_bbox), error = function(e) NULL)
  if (is.null(r)) {
    return(tibble(
      year = year, crop = crop_lut$crop,
      acres = NA_real_, percent_of_watershed = NA_real_
    ))
  }

  # 3) Project to equal-area, nearest neighbor preserves classes
  if (crs(r) != crs(ws_vect_aea)) {
    r <- project(r, crs(ws_vect_aea), method = "near")
  }

  # 4) Clip to watershed
  r <- mask(crop(r, ws_vect_aea), ws_vect_aea)

  # 5) Compute per-cell area in acres
  area_ac <- cellSize(r, unit = "ha") * 2.47105381

  # Total watershed acres (from rasterized AOI extent after masking)
  total_ac <- global(area_ac, sum, na.rm = TRUE)[1, 1]
  total_ac <- as.numeric(total_ac)

  # 6) For each crop code: sum area where r == code
  out <- crop_lut |>
    mutate(acres = map_dbl(cdl_code, function(code) {
      m <- (r == code)
      x <- global(area_ac * m, sum, na.rm = TRUE)[1, 1]
      as.numeric(x)
    })) |>
    transmute(
      year = year,
      crop = crop,
      acres = acres,
      percent_of_watershed = 100 * acres / total_ac
    )

  out
}
```


```{r}
# ----------------------------
# RUN TIME SERIES (long format)
# ----------------------------
ts_long <- map_dfr(
  years,
  crops_one_year,
  ws_sf_aea = ws_sf,
  ws_vect_aea = ws_vect,
  crop_lut = crop_lut,
  out_dir = out_dir
) |>
  arrange(year, crop)
```


```{r}
# ----------------------------
# SAVE CSV (long)
# ----------------------------
csv_long <- file.path(out_dir, "maumee_cdl_crops_timeseries_2008_present_long.csv")
write.csv(ts_long, csv_long, row.names = FALSE)
message("Saved long CSV: ", csv_long)

# ----------------------------
# SAVE CSV (wide: one column per crop)
# ----------------------------
ts_wide <- ts_long |>
  select(year, crop, acres) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = crop, values_from = acres)

csv_wide <- file.path(out_dir, "maumee_cdl_crops_timeseries_2008_present_wide.csv")
write.csv(ts_wide, csv_wide, row.names = FALSE)
message("Saved wide CSV: ", csv_wide)
```


```{r}
# ----------------------------
# PLOT: acres over time
# ----------------------------
ts_plot <- ts_long |>
  filter(!is.na(acres))

p <- ggplot(ts_plot, aes(x = year, y = acres, group = crop)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  facet_wrap(~ crop, scales = "free_y", ncol = 2) +
  labs(
    title = "Major crops in Maumee watershed (CDL)",
    subtitle = "Crops: Corn, Soybeans, Winter Wheat, Alfalfa â€” 2008 to present",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Acres"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

print(p)

plot_path <- file.path(out_dir, "maumee_major_crops_acres_faceted.png")
ggsave(plot_path, p, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 200)
message("Saved plot: ", plot_path)
```




```{r}
# ----------------------------
# OPTIONAL: also plot percent of watershed
# ----------------------------
p_pct <- ggplot(ts_plot, aes(x = year, y = percent_of_watershed, group = crop)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  facet_wrap(~ crop, scales = "free_y", ncol = 2) +
  labs(
    title = "Major crops as percent of Maumee watershed (CDL)",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Percent of watershed area (%)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

print(p_pct)

plot_pct_path <- file.path(out_dir, "maumee_major_crops_percent_faceted.png")
ggsave(plot_pct_path, p_pct, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 200)
message("Saved plot: ", plot_pct_path)
```

A single combined line plot (all crops on one axis) for the four top crops.


```{r}
# ----------------------------
# PLOT: acres over time (single combined line plot)
# ----------------------------
ts_plot <- ts_long |>
  filter(!is.na(acres))


# smooth outliers
ts_plot$acres[ts_plot$year==2019 & ts_plot$crop=='Corn'] = mean(c( ts_plot$acres[ts_plot$year==2018 & ts_plot$crop=='Corn']  , 
                                                                   ts_plot$acres[ts_plot$year==2020 & ts_plot$crop=='Corn'] ))
```

```{r}
ts_plot$acres[ts_plot$year==2019 & ts_plot$crop=='Soybeans'] = mean(c( ts_plot$acres[ts_plot$year==2018 & ts_plot$crop=='Soybeans']  , 
                                                                   ts_plot$acres[ts_plot$year==2020 & ts_plot$crop=='Soybeans'] ))

```


```{r}

ts_plot$crop= factor(ts_plot$crop,  levels=  c("Soybeans", "Corn", "Winter Wheat","Alfalfa" )) 


p_all <- ggplot(ts_plot, aes(x = year, y = acres, color = crop)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  labs(
    title = "Major crops in Maumee watershed (CDL)",
    subtitle = "Corn, Soybeans, Winter Wheat, Alfalfa â€” 2008 to present",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Acres",
    color = "Crop"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

print(p_all)

```

```{r}
ggplot(ts_plot, aes(x = year, y = acres, color = crop)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = unique(ts_plot$year)) +   # show every year
  labs(
    title = "Major crops in Maumee watershed (CDL)",
    subtitle = "Corn, Soybeans, Winter Wheat, Alfalfa â€” 2008 to present",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Acres",
    color = "Crop"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1, size = 8)
  )
```



```{r}

df <- read_csv( "Lake_erie/maumee_cdl_crops_timeseries_2008_to_2024_long.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
# expected columns: year, crop, acres
```


```{r}
# ---- Constant P2O5 rates (same method as before) ----
# NASS-style: avg lb P2O5 per planted acre = (%treated) * (lb/ac on treated acres)
rates <- tibble::tribble(
  ~crop,          ~pct_treated, ~rate_treated_lb_ac,
  "Corn",         0.79,         69.0,
  "Soybeans",     0.42,         55.0,
  "Winter Wheat", 0.63,         31.0,
  # Alfalfa: assumed constant "removal/maintenance" proxy (lb P2O5/ac)
  # using 39.6 lb/ac as in the previous method
  "Alfalfa",      1.00,         39.6
) %>%
  mutate(avg_lb_p2o5_per_ac = pct_treated * rate_treated_lb_ac)

LB_TO_METRIC_TON <- 0.00045359237

rates
```


```{r}
# ---- Compute annual P2O5 by crop and total ----
df_p <- df %>%
  left_join(rates %>% select(crop, avg_lb_p2o5_per_ac), by = "crop") %>%
  mutate(
    p2o5_lb = acres * avg_lb_p2o5_per_ac,
    p2o5_metric_ton = p2o5_lb * LB_TO_METRIC_TON
  )

annual_long <- df_p %>%
  group_by(year, crop) %>%
  summarise(p2o5_metric_ton = sum(p2o5_metric_ton, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

annual_total <- annual_long %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarise(total_p2o5_metric_ton = sum(p2o5_metric_ton, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

# Optional: wide table (one column per crop + Total)
annual_wide <- annual_long %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = crop, values_from = p2o5_metric_ton, values_fill = 0) %>%
  mutate(Total = rowSums(across(where(is.numeric), ~ .x), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  arrange(year)

# ---- Save outputs ----
#write_csv(annual_wide, "maumee_phosphate_use_timeseries_2008_2024_updated.csv")
```


```{r, fig.height=4,  fig.width=8}}
# ---- Plot 1: Total time series (line) ----
p_total <- ggplot(annual_total, aes(x = year, y = total_p2o5_metric_ton)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8, color='dodgerblue') +
  geom_point(size = 1.8) +
  scale_y_continuous( limits = c(7000,10500)) +
  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = "P2O5 applied (metric tons)",
    title = "Maumee watershed annual phosphate use (P2O5), 2008â€“2024",
    #subtitle = "Constant crop-specific rates Ă— CDL acres"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
p_total

#ggsave("maumee_p2o5_total_timeseries_2008_2024_updated.png", p_total,
#       width = 10, height = 4, dpi = 200)
```




```{r}
# ---- Plot 2: Stacked bar by crop ----
# Set a consistent crop order (optional)
annual_long$crop <- factor(annual_long$crop, levels = c("Corn", "Soybeans", "Winter Wheat", "Alfalfa"))

p_stack <- ggplot(annual_long, aes(x = year, y = p2o5_metric_ton, fill = crop)) +
  geom_col() +
   labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = "P2O5 applied (metric tons)",
    fill = "Crop",
    title = "Maumee watershed annual phosphate use (P2O5) by crop, 2008â€“2024"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "right")

p_stack
#ggsave("maumee_p2o5_by_crop_stacked_2008_2024_updated.png", p_stack,
#       width = 10, height = 4.5, dpi = 200)
```


```{r, fig.height=4, fig.width=8}
df_long <- annual_wide %>%
  select(year, Corn, Soybeans, `Winter Wheat`, Alfalfa) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -year,
    names_to = "crop",
    values_to = "p2o5_metric_ton"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    # set a consistent stacking/legend order (bottom -> top)
    crop = factor(crop, levels = rev(c("Corn", "Soybeans", "Winter Wheat", "Alfalfa")))
  )

# ---- Stacked AREA plot (matches your figure style) ----
p_stack_area <- ggplot(df_long, aes(x = year, y = p2o5_metric_ton, fill = crop)) +
  geom_area(alpha = 0.95) +
  labs(
    title = "Estimated phosphate (P2O5) fertilizer use by crop (2008â€“2024)",
    #subtitle = "Assuming constant crop-specific rates",
    x = "Year",
    y = "P2O5 applied (metric tons)",
    fill = "Crop"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position = "right"
  )

p_stack_area

# Optional: save to file
#ggsave("maumee_p2o5_by_crop_stacked_area_2008_2024.png",
#       p_stack_area, width = 12, height = 6, dpi = 200)

```


```{r}
## merge with TP data to check Phosphate use with TP load 
Flow_HAB6= read.csv("Flow_HAB6.csv")
```


```{r}
annual_total$year= annual_total$year-1

Flow_HAB4=  merge( annual_total, Flow_HAB6,  by="year", all=T)
```




```{r}
corp <- df %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = crop, values_from = acres) %>%
  arrange(year)

corp$year= corp$year-1

## merge with TP data to check Phosphate use with TP load 


Flow_HAB5=  merge( corp, Flow_HAB4,  by="year", all=T)
```


```{r}
#annual_avg_data= rename(annual_avg_data, year= Year)

Corp_flow=merge( corp, annual_total,  by="year", all=T)
```
  
  
  

### Positively correlated

```{r, fig.width=6, fig.height=5, warning= FALSE, message=FALSE}
### plot scatter plot and check correlation
## corp data is 1 year lagged

  ggplot(data=Flow_HAB4[!is.na(Flow_HAB4$total_p2o5_metric_ton ),  ], aes(x= total_p2o5_metric_ton , y=severity_index     ) ) +
      geom_point(alpha=0.6, size=2, col=2) +
      labs(x= "Phosphate use in the previous year (metric tons)", y= "HAB severity",
           title= "HAB Severity ~ Phosphate fertilizer (1 year lagged) ")+
        geom_smooth(method='lm', se=F)+  theme_classic(base_size = 13) + 
      geom_text(aes(label = year), 
            hjust = 0.3, vjust = -0.3, size = 4) +   # adjust position as needed
       stat_cor(method2 = 'pearson', size=5) 


```
